input.prompt:
[CONTEXT] Your previous pass started Glide feasibility research for a narrow qutebrowser-only macOS workflow, but I still need the final evidence-based conclusion. The user wants: Cmd-Enter in qutebrowser -> automatic side-by-side with a second qutebrowser window -> close second window -> first window auto-expands back to full width. They do NOT want system-wide tiling or noticeable interference with other apps.

[TASK] Finish the Glide feasibility research and return a concrete conclusion.

[EXPECTED OUTCOME] A concise but evidence-rich summary covering: 1) what Glide can scope by app/window/rules; 2) whether it can automate creating a second qutebrowser window vs only arranging existing windows; 3) whether it can detect one window closing and re-expand the survivor automatically; 4) whether focus hotkeys can be limited to this exact scenario; 5) overall verdict: suitable / awkward / unsuitable for this MVP.

[REQUIRED TOOLS] Web/docs/issues/references only.

[MUST DO] Include concrete sources by name or URL when possible. Distinguish confirmed capabilities from speculation. If Glide is the wrong fit, say so clearly and explain why.

[MUST NOT DO] Do not give generic macOS window manager overviews. Do not pad with unrelated features. Do not leave the answer at a draft/introduction stage.

[CONTEXT] This is research only; no implementation yet.
